observed_facts: All 6 episodes triggered r_landing_success (terminal reward paid rate 1.0) but success episode rate is 0.0 because landings are not stable/safe
High contact toggling: mean 106.8, max 140, indicating bouncing or intermittent ground contact
r_center_guidance mean 0.13, max 0.498 – lateral positioning is suboptimal
r_descent_guidance mean 0.305 (1 - y), implying average y ≈ 0.695 – descent is not aggressive
r_fuel_penalty mean -0.03 – fuel cost is moderate
Hack score 0.2 with shaping_goal_mismatch flag true, no reward hacking
likely_true_failures: Insufficient incentive for soft, slow touchdown – r_vertical_safety only activates when y < 0.1 and penalises |vy|, but does not encourage gentle descent overall
Weak lateral centering – r_center_guidance weight too low to adequately pull the lander to x=0
Descent speed is not penalised – agent can descend quickly and bounce, as there is no cost for high vx or vy away from ground
Fuel efficiency could be improved by slightly increasing fuel penalty
likely_false_positives: The terminal_success_dominance flag (component ratio 0.512) is benign – landing is the ultimate goal, and dense shaping is still present
Unstable contact toggling is not reward hacking; it is a task performance issue
root_cause_hypotheses: The dense reward components are not sufficiently aligned with the desired soft-landing behaviour: centering is too weak, descent incentive exists but does not penalise speed, and vertical safety only operates very close to ground
The r_vertical_safety condition is too restrictive (only y < 0.1) and the penalty is only on vy; a broader speed penalty or continuous vertical control feedback could stabilise touchdown
Fuel cost is mild and could be increased to discourage inefficient hovering/thrust

lesson_assessments.reason: Previous edit successfully reduced center dominance and added descent guidance, proving that coupled rebalancing can improve task outcomes without hacking.

reusable_lessons: Decrease dominant component weight and introduce missing dense progress shaping
Coupled edits (weight adjustments + new shaping) are more reliable than single changes
avoid_actions: Removing or severely weakening centering guidance
Making terminal success the sole strong reward without intermediate dense shaping
Drastically increasing any single component weight to dominance (>0.8 ratio)
recommended_actions: Increase weight of r_center_guidance and r_descent_guidance moderately
Add a continuous speed penalty (vx and vy) to dampen bouncing

reward_editor.rationale: To improve landing quality, strengthen centering and descent incentives, and add a continuous speed penalty to reduce bouncing, while keeping safety constraints intact. Previous memory supports this coupled approach.
reward_editor.expected_effect: Agent should land more centered, descend faster, and with lower speeds; contact toggling should decrease; success rate may remain high but landing stability should improve.
reward_editor.risk_analysis: If centering or descent weights are too high, they could overshadow other objectives; speed penalty might discourage necessary descent velocity; but moderate increases (1.5×) and a mild penalty weight (0.3) limit this risk. Monitoring after training will detect any new dominance or crash increase.

what_worked: Moderate weight increases on centering and descent components
Adding a continuous speed penalty with clip [-1,0]
avoid_next: Overweighting any single dense component to dominance
recommend_next: Monitor contact toggles and adjust speed penalty scaling if needed
Consider further incremental weight tuning if landing remains unstable
applicability_notes: Applicable when terminal reward is achieved but landing quality is poor (bouncing, weak centering, slow descent).

diagnosis: Poor landing quality due to weak centering, slow descent, and no speed penalty leads to bouncing and off-center landings. Edits strengthen these aspects while preserving safety.
